marketFocus: Anthropic presentation eases AI disruption concerns, Meta-AMD USD 60bln chip deal boosts tech sentiment
Trump State of the Union: 'Nation is back, bigger, better and stronger', tariffs remain despite Supreme Court ruling
Australian CPI hotter than expected (3.8% vs 3.7%), RBA Trimmed Mean CPI 3.4% vs 3.3%
Today: German GDP Final, EZ HICP Final, RBA's Bullock speaks, NVIDIA earnings

USD.name: USD
USD.summary: DXY mildly softer but supported by Trump's State of the Union rhetoric ('nation is back, bigger, better and stronger') and Fed's hawkish hold stance. Collins (2028 voter) said 'quite likely to hold current rates for some time', while Barkin (2027 voter) noted inflation data 'consistently above target'. Geopolitical premium persists with Iran tensions simmering despite diplomatic overtures.
drivers: Trump State of the Union: tariffs remain, economy 'roaring like never before'
Fed's Collins: quite likely to hold rates for some time, policy mildly restrictive
Fed's Barkin: inflation data consistently above target, wants more evidence of retreat to 2%
Iran tensions: Trump says Iran working on missiles that could reach US, prefers diplomacy
Today: Crude Oil Inventories (exp +1.8M)

EUR.name: EUR
EUR.summary: EUR/USD eked mild gains to retest 1.1800 amid lacklustre dollar and after EU's Sefcovic comments that US counterparts reassured the EU they will stand by the trade deal. German Chancellor Merz in Beijing, stating decoupling from China would hamper economic opportunities. Focus shifts to German GDP Final and EZ HICP Final data today.
drivers: EUR/USD retesting 1.1800 level, supported by trade deal reassurance
EU's Sefcovic: US counterparts reassured EU they will stand by trade deal
German Chancellor Merz in Beijing, warns against China decoupling
Bund futures lacked direction ahead of German data
Today: German GDP Final (Q4), German GfK Consumer Climate, EZ HICP Final

GBP.name: GBP
GBP.summary: GBP/USD gradually rebounded after prior day's fluctuations and deluge of BoE remarks. Main takeaway: April/May cut remains an open question, with Governor Bailey stating he will be 'going into the coming meetings asking if a cut is justified'. UK Chancellor Reeves facing renewed calls to cut bank tax as UK competitiveness lags.
drivers: GBP/USD rebounding after BoE remarks, April/May cut open question
BoE Governor Bailey: will ask if cut is justified in coming meetings
UK Chancellor Reeves facing calls to cut bank tax amid competitiveness concerns
Gilts slightly softer, underperforming Bunds
Today: No major UK data scheduled

JPY.name: JPY
JPY.summary: USD/JPY mildly pulled back after surging yesterday on report that Japanese PM Takaichi relayed to BoJ Governor Ueda reservations about further rate hikes, being stricter than their previous meeting. Takaichi commented she is 'closely watching FX moves with a high sense of urgency'. Former BoJ Governor Kuroda says Japan needs to move toward tighter fiscal and monetary policy as economy is 'already in great shape'.
drivers: USD/JPY surged on Takaichi's reservations about BoJ rate hikes (stricter than before)
Japanese PM Takaichi: closely watching FX moves with high sense of urgency
Former BoJ Governor Kuroda: Japan needs tighter fiscal/monetary policy
Japan nominates professors Asada and Sato to replace outgoing BoJ board members
10yr JGB futures faded gains after enhanced-liquidity auction

AUD.name: AUD
AUD.summary: AUD/USD leading Antipodean advances following firmer-than-expected monthly CPI data (3.8% vs exp 3.7%, RBA Trimmed Mean CPI 3.4% vs exp 3.3%). ASX 200 advanced with gains led by tech, consumer staples and mining sectors, unfazed by hotter inflation. Positive risk appetite and AI disruption concerns easing provide tailwinds. RBA Governor Bullock speaks today.
drivers: Australian CPI 3.8% vs exp 3.7%, RBA Trimmed Mean CPI 3.4% vs exp 3.3%
AUD/USD leading Antipodean gains amid positive risk appetite
ASX 200 rallied, tech and mining sectors outperforming
Risk-on sentiment: Anthropic presentation eased AI concerns
Today: RBA Governor Bullock speaks

CAD.name: CAD
CAD.summary: CAD choppy and pressured amid ongoing USMCA uncertainty. Trump's State of the Union confirmed tariffs will remain in place despite Supreme Court ruling, with 'nearly all countries want to keep the trade deals'. Canadian PM Carney reportedly knew of South Bow's plans for partial Keystone XL revival when he floated pipeline idea to Trump.
drivers: Trump State of the Union: tariffs remain, Supreme Court ruling 'very unfortunate'
USMCA uncertainty persists, Canadian officials cite 'very low' renewal expectations
Canadian PM Carney knew of South Bow's Keystone XL revival plans
Oil choppy: private inventory data showed crude +11.4M (exp +1.5M)
Today: No major CAD data scheduled

CHF.name: CHF
CHF.summary: CHF mixed, benefiting from safe-haven flows amid geopolitical tensions but pressured by EUR weakness. SNB Chairman Schlegel said Swiss inflationary pressure has 'barely changed', central bank expects growth around 1% in 2026, and inflation will rise in next few months. Possible negative inflation months ahead but 'not an alarm signal'.
drivers: SNB Chairman Schlegel: inflationary pressure barely changed, growth ~1% in 2026
SNB expects inflation to rise in coming months, possible negative months ahead
Safe-haven flows support CHF amid Iran tensions
EUR weakness weighs on CHF crosses
Today: No major CHF data scheduled

NZD.name: NZD
NZD.summary: NZD firmer alongside AUD amid positive risk appetite and AI disruption concerns easing. Antipodeans benefiting from broad risk-on sentiment after Anthropic presentation and Meta-AMD chip deal. China markets open, with PBoC conducting CNY 600bln MLF operation and Chinese provinces raising technology spending in 2026 budgets.
drivers: NZD following AUD higher amid positive risk appetite
Risk-on sentiment: Anthropic eased AI concerns, Meta-AMD chip deal
China markets open: PBoC CNY 600bln MLF operation
Chinese provinces raising tech spending in 2026 budgets (Guangdong, Zhejiang, Hainan)
Today: No major NZD data scheduled

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-note futures slightly lower after choppy performance, with curve flattening as AI disruption fears were allayed by Anthropic presentation. Little reaction seen during Trump's State of the Union Address. Fed's Collins and Barkin reiterated hawkish hold stance, limiting upside for bonds.
drivers: T-note futures slightly lower after curve flattening
AI disruption fears eased by Anthropic presentation
Fed's Collins: quite likely to hold rates for some time
Fed's Barkin: inflation data consistently above target
Today: Crude Oil Inventories

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bund futures lacked direction following prior day's indecision, with Bund issuances and German GfK data scheduled today. EUR/USD retesting 1.1800 provides mild support. German Chancellor Merz in Beijing, warning against China decoupling.
drivers: Bund futures lacked direction ahead of German data
Bund issuances scheduled today
EUR/USD retesting 1.1800 level
German Chancellor Merz in Beijing
Today: German GDP Final, GfK Consumer Climate

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures faded some of prior day's gains that were facilitated by reports that Japanese PM Takaichi relayed to BoJ Governor Ueda her reservations about further rate hikes. Headwinds seen following latest enhanced-liquidity auction for long- to super-long JGBs.
drivers: JGB futures faded gains after enhanced-liquidity auction
Takaichi's reservations about BoJ rate hikes (stricter than before)
Former BoJ Governor Kuroda: Japan needs tighter policy
Japan nominates new BoJ board members (Asada, Sato)
USD/JPY surged on Takaichi comments

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts slightly softer and marginally underperforming Bunds. BoE Governor Bailey stated he will be 'going into the coming meetings asking if a cut is justified', keeping April/May cut as open question. UK Chancellor Reeves facing renewed calls to cut bank tax as UK competitiveness lags.
drivers: Gilts slightly softer, underperforming Bunds
BoE Governor Bailey: will ask if cut is justified in coming meetings
UK Chancellor Reeves facing calls to cut bank tax
GBP/USD rebounding after BoE remarks
Today: No major UK data scheduled
